body:
## Summary

Fixes #33.

The reporter ran `cargo run` several times on the live site and saw two branded lines — `This binary was compiled inside CheerpX.` and `Compiled by Rust inside the browser via CheerpX.` — that "look like something we added in our code," and asked us to confirm the build is not faked and to make the box behave 1-to-1 with a real terminal.

**The build is genuinely real.** `cargo run` is a real `rustc` invocation inside the CheerpX x86 Linux VM — not a canned transcript. The proof is in the reporter's own output: every post-edit run prints `Compiling hello v0.1.0 (/workspace)`, the wall-clock times vary realistically (32.81s → 8m 19s → 2.07s → 2.02s), and the reporter's edits (`! 2`, `! 3`, `! 5`) appear in stdout — none of which a mocked terminal could produce.

**The real defect** was that the greeting was branded *and inconsistent across sources*. The prebuilt binary baked by `web/disk/Dockerfile.disk` printed `This binary was compiled inside CheerpX.`, while the editable workspace seed in `web/glue/workspace-fs.js` printed `Compiled by Rust inside the browser via CheerpX.`. So the **first** run (prebuilt) and **later** runs (recompiled from the seed) printed *different* second lines. That divergence is what made a real build look injected.

**The fix** removes the branding and makes both the prebuilt binary's source and the editable seed byte-for-byte identical to the canonical `cargo new` program:

```rust
fn main() {
    println!("Hello, world!");
}
```

Now the first run equals every later run and the box behaves exactly like a freshly created Cargo project — "1 to 1 to real deal." Existing browser workspaces that still hold the untouched branded seed are migrated to the plain program on next open; user edits are preserved.

## Reproduction

On the live Pages site, open the terminal and run `cargo run`. The first run reuses the prebuilt disk binary and prints `Hello from rust-web-box!` / `This binary was compiled inside CheerpX.`. Edit `src/main.rs` and run `cargo run` again: it really recompiles (`Compiling hello…`) and prints the *other* branded line, `Compiled by Rust inside the browser via CheerpX.`. The two different marketing-style lines for the same project are the "fake-looking" smell.

Evidence is preserved in `docs/case-studies/issue-33/`, including the annotated terminal transcript (`evidence/issue-terminal-transcript.txt`), the reporter screenshot (`screenshots/issue-terminal.png`), and the published warm-disk release metadata.

## Changes

- `web/glue/workspace-fs.js`: the editable seed `src/main.rs` is now the plain `cargo new` program (no comment header, single `println!`). The old branded text is pinned in `PREVIOUS_SEED_FILES`, and `migrateDefaultSettings()` calls `replaceIfUnchanged()` so untouched branded workspaces migrate to the plain program while user edits are left alone.
- `web/disk/Dockerfile.disk`: the prebuilt binary's `src/main.rs` is byte-for-byte identical to the seed, so the first (prebuilt) run matches every later run. The dual debug + release pre-bake (issue #17 / #31 mitigation) is preserved.
- `.github/workflows/disk-image.yml`: the mounted-image smoke test asserts `Hello, world!` and no longer asserts the removed CheerpX branding line.
- `web/tests/workspace-fs.test.mjs`: asserts the seed is the canonical program and carries no `rust-web-box` / `CheerpX` / `Compiled by Rust` branding.
- `web/tests/workspace-fs-migration.test.mjs` (new): an in-memory IndexedDB shim drives `openWorkspaceFS()` to prove an untouched branded seed is migrated to the plain program, a user-edited `main.rs` is left untouched, and a fresh store is seeded with the plain program.
- `web/tests/boot-shell.test.mjs`: the disk-image workflow assertion matches `Hello, world!` and asserts the branding strings are absent.
- `web/tests/helpers/cheerpx-page-harness.mjs`: adds `firstSourceGreeting(page)`, which reads `/workspace/src/main.rs` and extracts the first `println!` literal.
- `web/tests/e2e/local-pages-e2e.test.mjs` and `live-pages-e2e.test.mjs`: Stage C/D assert that the greeting printed by the binary and by `cargo run` is exactly the literal in the source on disk (a stronger anti-fake check than a hardcoded constant). The strict de-branding assertion is gated on a **`prebuiltBranded` discriminator** read from the prebuilt binary's own output (`/workspace/target/release/hello`): that binary is baked into the disk and is unaffected by the boot-time workspace prime or CheerpX's guest clock, so it reliably tells the issue #33 plain-seed disk apart from a previously published branded disk. This replaces the earlier `hasLeanCargoDevProfile(page)` gate, which produced a false positive — the lean dev profile shipped in issue #31 and is already published, so it was `true` on the still-branded `disk-latest` and fired `assert.equal(greeting, 'Hello, world!')` against `Hello from rust-web-box!`, failing the Deploy GitHub Pages "Local e2e (built artifact)" job. The new gate keeps the suite green during the version-skew window and auto-enables strict enforcement on the Pages job the moment the de-branded disk is published.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-33/`: full case study (timeline, requirements, root cause, solution options, online research) plus evidence, screenshot, and verification logs.
- `changelog.d/20260527_093732_issue_33_remove_fake_branding.md`: changelog fragment (patch bump).

No upstream issue was filed: the CheerpX/webvm runtime behaves correctly here; the defect was entirely in this repository's seed/disk content.

## Tests

- `node --test web/tests/` → 197 pass, 4 e2e skipped (no browser locally)
- `node --test web/tests/workspace-fs.test.mjs web/tests/workspace-fs-migration.test.mjs`
- `node --test web/tests/boot-shell.test.mjs`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo test`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs`

The full fixed-disk browser e2e assertion runs in `.github/workflows/disk-image.yml`, because the normal PR Pages workflow stages the currently published `disk-latest` image rather than the disk built by this PR.
title: Remove fake-looking branding; make in-browser cargo run 1-to-1 with a real terminal
